1.author: Ahmed Ali
1.text: I CALL TO witness the sky (bespangled) with the signs of the Zodiac,

1.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
1.text: By oath of the heaven which contains the constellations.

1.author: Daryabadi
1.text: By the heaven adorned with big stars.

1.author: Hilali & Khan
1.text: By the heaven, holding the big stars.

1.author: Itani
1.text: By the sky with the constellations.

1.author: Maududi
1.text: By the heaven with its impregnable castles;

1.author: Mubarakpuri
1.text: By the heaven holding the Buruj.

1.author: Pickthall
1.text: By the heaven, holding mansions of the stars,

1.author: Qarai
1.text: By the heaven with its Houses,

1.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
1.text: By the heaven of the constellations!

1.author: Saheeh International
1.text: By the sky containing great stars

1.author: Shakir
1.text: I swear by the mansions of the stars,

1.author: Wahiduddin Khan
1.text: By the sky with its constellations,

1.author: Yusuf Ali
1.text: By the sky, (displaying) the Zodiacal Signs;

2.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
2.text: And by oath of the Promised Day.

2.author: Hilali & Khan
2.text: And by the Promised Day (i.e. the Day of Resurrection);

2.author: Mubarakpuri
2.text: And by by the Promised Day.

3.author: Ahmed Ali
3.text: And the witness and the witnessed,

3.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
3.text: And by oath of the day that is a witness and by oath of a day in which the people present themselves.

3.author: Arberry
3.text: by the witness and the witnessed,

3.author: Daryabadi
3.text: And by the Day witnessing and the Day witnessed,

3.author: Hilali & Khan
3.text: And by the witnessing day (i.e. Friday), and by the witnessed day [i.e. the day of 'Arafat (Hajj) the ninth of Dhul-Hijjah];

3.author: Itani
3.text: And by the witness and the witnessed.

3.author: Maududi
3.text: and by the witness and what is witnessed:

3.author: Mubarakpuri
3.text: And by the Witness and by the Witnessed.

3.author: Pickthall
3.text: And by the witness and that whereunto he beareth testimony,

3.author: Qarai
3.text: by the Witness and the Witnessed:

3.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
3.text: By the witness and the witnessed!

3.author: Saheeh International
3.text: And [by] the witness and what is witnessed,

3.author: Sarwar
3.text: and by the witness (Muhammad) and that which is witnessed (the Day of Judgment),

3.author: Shakir
3.text: And the bearer of witness and those against whom the witness is borne.

3.author: Wahiduddin Khan
3.text: by the Witness and the witnessed,

3.author: Yusuf Ali
3.text: By one that witnesses, and the subject of the witness;-

4.author: Ahmed Ali
4.text: That accursed are the men of the trench

4.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
4.text: Accursed be the People of the Ditch!

4.author: Arberry
4.text: slain were the Men of the Pit,

4.author: Daryabadi
4.text: Perish the fellows of the ditch,

4.author: Hilali & Khan
4.text: Cursed were the people of the ditch (the story of the Boy and the King).

4.author: Itani
4.text: Destroyed were the People of the Trench.

4.author: Maududi
4.text: the people of the pit were destroyed

4.author: Mubarakpuri
4.text: Cursed were the People of the Ditch.

4.author: Pickthall
4.text: (Self-)destroyed were the owners of the ditch

4.author: Qarai
4.text: perish the People of the Ditch!

4.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
4.text: The companions of the pit were killed

4.author: Saheeh International
4.text: Cursed were the companions of the trench

4.author: Sarwar
4.text: may the people be condemned those who tortured (the believers) in ditches

4.author: Shakir
4.text: Cursed be the makers of the pit,

4.author: Wahiduddin Khan
4.text: destroyed were the people of the trench,

4.author: Yusuf Ali
4.text: Woe to the makers of the pit (of fire),

5.author: Ahmed Ali
5.text: Full of fire fed by faggots,

5.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
5.text: The people of the fuelled blazing fire.

5.author: Shakir
5.text: Of the fire (kept burning) with fuel,

5.author: Wahiduddin Khan
5.text: the makers of the fuel-stoked fire!

6.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
6.text: When they were sitting at its edge.

6.author: Arberry
6.text: when they were seated over it

6.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
6.text: when they were seated around it

6.author: Saheeh International
6.text: When they were sitting near it

6.author: Sarwar
6.text: while they themselves sat around it

6.author: Yusuf Ali
6.text: Behold! they sat over against the (fire),

7.author: Ahmed Ali
7.text: Witnessing what they had done to the believers!

7.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
7.text: And they themselves are witnesses to what they were doing to the Muslims!

7.author: Arberry
7.text: and were themselves witnesses of what they did with the believers.

7.author: Daryabadi
7.text: And to that which they did to the believers were witnesses.

7.author: Hilali & Khan
7.text: And they witnessed what they were doing against the believers (i.e. burning them).

7.author: Itani
7.text: And were witnessing what they did to the believers.

7.author: Maududi
7.text: and were witnessing what they did to the believers.

7.author: Mubarakpuri
7.text: And they witnessed what they were doing against the believers.

7.author: Pickthall
7.text: And were themselves the witnesses of what they did to the believers.

7.author: Qarai
7.text: as they were themselves witnesses to what they did to the faithful.

7.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
7.text: and they were witnesses of what they did to the believers

7.author: Saheeh International
7.text: And they, to what they were doing against the believers, were witnesses.

7.author: Shakir
7.text: And they were witnesses of what they did with the believers.

7.author: Wahiduddin Khan
7.text: to watch what they were doing to the believers,

7.author: Yusuf Ali
7.text: And they witnessed (all) that they were doing against the Believers.

7.author: Transliteration
7.text: Wahum AAala ma yafAAaloona bialmumineena shuhoodun

8.author: Ahmed Ali
8.text: They had no other reason for hating them except that they believed in God the mighty, worthy of praise,

8.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
8.text: And what did they dislike from the Muslims, except that the Muslims accepted faith in Allah the Most Honourable, the Most Praiseworthy?

8.author: Arberry
8.text: They took revenge on them only because they believed in the All-mighty, the Al-laudable,

8.author: Daryabadi
8.text: And they persecuted them for naught save that they believed in Allah, the Mighty, the Praiseworthy

8.author: Hilali & Khan
8.text: They had nothing against them, except that they believed in Allah, the All-Mighty, Worthy of all Praise!

8.author: Itani
8.text: They begrudged them only because they believed in God the Almighty, the Praiseworthy.

8.author: Maududi
8.text: Against these they had no grudge except that they believed in Allah, the Most Mighty, the Most Praiseworthy,

8.author: Mubarakpuri
8.text: And they had no fault except that they believed in Allah, the Almighty, Worthy of all praise!

8.author: Pickthall
8.text: They had naught against them save that they believed in Allah, the Mighty, the Owner of Praise,

8.author: Qarai
8.text: They were vindictive towards them only because they had faith in Allah, the All-mighty and the All-laudable,

8.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
8.text: and their revenge on them was only because they believed in Allah, the Almighty, the Praised,

8.author: Saheeh International
8.text: And they resented them not except because they believed in Allah, the Exalted in Might, the Praiseworthy,

8.author: Sarwar
8.text: The only reason for which they tormented the believers was the latter's belief in God, the Majestic, and Praiseworthy.

8.author: Shakir
8.text: And they did not take vengeance on them for aught except that they believed in Allah, the Mighty, the Praised,

8.author: Wahiduddin Khan
8.text: whom they hated for no other reason than that they believed in God, the Almighty, the Praiseworthy,

8.author: Yusuf Ali
8.text: And they ill-treated them for no other reason than that they believed in Allah, Exalted in Power, Worthy of all Praise!-

8.author: Transliteration
8.text: Wama naqamoo minhum illa an yuminoo biAllahi alAAazeezi alhameedi

9.author: Ahmed Ali
9.text: Whose kingdom spreads over the heavens and the earth. God is witness over everything.

9.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
9.text: To Him only belongs the kingship of the heavens and the earth; and Allah is a Witness over all things.

9.author: Arberry
9.text: God to whom belongs the Kingdom of the heavens and the earth, and God is Witness over everything.

9.author: Daryabadi
9.text: Him, Whose is the dominion of heavens and the earth. And Allah is over everything a Witness.

9.author: Hilali & Khan
9.text: Who, to Whom belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth! And Allah is Witness over everything.

9.author: Itani
9.text: To Whom belongs the sovereignty of the heavens and the earth. God is witness over everything.

9.author: Maududi
9.text: to Whom belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth. Allah witnesses everything.

9.author: Mubarakpuri
9.text: To Whom belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth! And Allah is Witness over everything.

9.author: Pickthall
9.text: Him unto Whom belongeth the Sovereignty of the heavens and the earth; and Allah is of all things the Witness.

9.author: Qarai
9.text: to whom belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth, and Allah is witness to all things.

9.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
9.text: to whom belongs the Kingdom of the heavens and earth. And Allah is the Witness of all things.

9.author: Saheeh International
9.text: To whom belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth. And Allah, over all things, is Witness.

9.author: Sarwar
9.text: and the One to whom belongs the heavens and the earth. God is the Witness of all things.

9.author: Shakir
9.text: Whose is the kingdom of the heavens and the earth; and Allah is a Witness of all things.

9.author: Wahiduddin Khan
9.text: to whom belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. God is witness over all things.

9.author: Yusuf Ali
9.text: Him to Whom belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth! And Allah is Witness to all things.

9.author: Transliteration
9.text: Allathee lahu mulku alssamawati waalardi waAllahu AAala kulli shayin shaheedun

10.author: Ahmed Ali
10.text: Surely for those who persecute believers, men and women, and do not repent afterwards, is the punishment of Hell, and the punishment of burning.

10.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
10.text: Indeed those who troubled the Muslim men and Muslim women, and then did not repent – for them is the punishment of hell, and for them is the punishment of fire.

10.author: Arberry
10.text: Those who persecute the believers, men and women, and then have not repented, there awaits them the chastisement of Gehenna, and there awaits them the chastisement of the burning.

10.author: Daryabadi
10.text: Verily those who affected the believing men and the believing women, and then repented not, theirs shall be the torment of Hell, and theirs shall be the torment of burning.

10.author: Hilali & Khan
10.text: Verily, those who put into trial the believing men and believing women (by torturing them and burning them), and then do not turn in repentance, (to Allah), will have the torment of Hell, and they will have the punishment of the burning Fire.

10.author: Itani
10.text: Those who tempt the believers, men and women, then do not repent; for them is the punishment of Hell; for them is the punishment of Burning.

10.author: Maududi
10.text: Surely those who tormented the believing men and the believing women and then did not repent, theirs shall be the chastisement of Hell, and theirs shall be the chastisement of burning.

10.author: Mubarakpuri
10.text: Verily, those who put into trial the believing men and believing women, and then do not turn in repentance, then they will have the torment of Hell, and they will have the punishment of the burning Fire.

10.author: Pickthall
10.text: Lo! they who persecute believing men and believing women and repent not, theirs verily will be the doom of hell, and theirs the doom of burning.

10.author: Qarai
10.text: Indeed those who persecute the faithful, men and women, and do not repent thereafter, there is the punishment of hell for them and the punishment of burning.

10.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
10.text: Those who persecuted the believing men and women and never repented, there is for them the punishment of Gehenna (Hell), and for them the punishment of the burning.

10.author: Saheeh International
10.text: Indeed, those who have tortured the believing men and believing women and then have not repented will have the punishment of Hell, and they will have the punishment of the Burning Fire.

10.author: Sarwar
10.text: Those who persecute the believing men and women without repenting will suffer the torment of hell and that of the burning fire.

10.author: Shakir
10.text: Surely (as for) those who persecute the believing men and the believing women, then do not repent, they shall have the chastisement of hell, and they shall have the chastisement of burning.

10.author: Wahiduddin Khan
10.text: Those who persecute the believing men and believing women, and then do not repent, will surely suffer the punishment of Hell, and the torment of burning.

10.author: Yusuf Ali
10.text: Those who persecute (or draw into temptation) the Believers, men and women, and do not turn in repentance, will have the Penalty of Hell: They will have the Penalty of the Burning Fire.

10.author: Transliteration
10.text: Inna allatheena fatanoo almumineena waalmuminati thumma lam yatooboo falahum AAathabu jahannama walahum AAathabu alhareeqi

11.author: Ahmed Ali
11.text: Surely for those who believe and do the right are gardens with rivers running by. That is the greatest success.

11.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
11.text: Indeed those who believed and did good deeds – for them are Gardens beneath which rivers flow; this is the great success.

11.author: Arberry
11.text: Those who believe, and do righteous deeds, for them await gardens underneath which rivers flow; that is the great triumph.

11.author: Daryabadi
11.text: Verily those who believed and worked righteons works, theirs shall be Gardens whereunder the rivers flow; that is the supreme achievement.

11.author: Hilali & Khan
11.text: Verily, those who believe and do righteous good deeds, for them will be Gardens under which rivers flow (Paradise). That is the great success.

11.author: Itani
11.text: Those who believe and do righteous deeds will have Gardens beneath which rivers flow. That is the great triumph.

11.author: Maududi
11.text: As for those who believed and acted righteously, theirs shall be Gardens beneath which rivers flow. That is the great triumph.

11.author: Mubarakpuri
11.text: Verily, those who believe and do righteous good deeds, for them will be Gardens under which rivers flow. That is the supreme success.

11.author: Pickthall
11.text: Lo! those who believe and do good works, theirs will be Gardens underneath which rivers flow. That is the Great Success.

11.author: Qarai
11.text: Indeed those who have faith and do righteous deeds—for them will be gardens with streams running in them. That is the supreme success.

11.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
11.text: (But) for those who believe and do good works, for them there are Gardens underneath which rivers flow that is the Great Victory!

11.author: Saheeh International
11.text: Indeed, those who have believed and done righteous deeds will have gardens beneath which rivers flow. That is the great attainment.

11.author: Sarwar
11.text: As for the righteously striving believers, they will live in Paradise wherein streams flow. This is the greatest triumph.

11.author: Shakir
11.text: Surely (as for) those who believe and do good, they shall have gardens beneath which rivers flow, that is the great achievement.

11.author: Wahiduddin Khan
11.text: But those who believe and do good deeds shall be rewarded with gardens watered by flowing rivers. That is the supreme triumph.

11.author: Yusuf Ali
11.text: For those who believe and do righteous deeds, will be Gardens; beneath which rivers flow: That is the great Salvation, (the fulfilment of all desires),

11.author: Transliteration
11.text: Inna allatheena amanoo waAAamiloo alssalihati lahum jannatun tajree min tahtiha alanharu thalika alfawzu alkabeeru

12.author: Ahmed Ali
12.text: The grip of your Lord is severe indeed!

12.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
12.text: Indeed the seizure of your Lord is very severe.

12.author: Arberry
12.text: Surely thy Lord's assault is terrible.

12.author: Daryabadi
12.text: Verily the grip of thine Lord is severe.

12.author: Hilali & Khan
12.text: Verily, (O Muhammad (Peace be upon him)) the Grip (Punishment) of your Lord is severe.

12.author: Itani
12.text: The onslaught of your Lord is severe.

12.author: Maududi
12.text: Stern indeed is your Lord's punishment.

12.author: Mubarakpuri
12.text: Verily, the punishment of your Lord is severe and painful.

12.author: Pickthall
12.text: Lo! the punishment of thy Lord is stern.

12.author: Qarai
12.text: Indeed your Lord’s striking is severe.

12.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
12.text: Indeed, the seizing of your Lord is severe.

12.author: Saheeh International
12.text: Indeed, the vengeance of your Lord is severe.

12.author: Sarwar
12.text: The vengeance of God is terribly severe.

12.author: Shakir
12.text: Surely the might of your Lord is great.

12.author: Wahiduddin Khan
12.text: The grip of your Lord is indeed severe,

12.author: Yusuf Ali
12.text: Truly strong is the Grip (and Power) of thy Lord.

13.author: Ahmed Ali
13.text: It is verily He who initiates and repeats.

13.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
13.text: Indeed it is He Who initiates and redoes.

13.author: Arberry
13.text: Surely it is He who originates, and brings again,

13.author: Daryabadi
13.text: Verily He! He it is who beginneth and repeateth it.

13.author: Hilali & Khan
13.text: Verily, He it is Who begins (punishment) and repeats (punishment in the Hereafter) (or originates the creation of everything, and then repeats it on the Day of Resurrection).

13.author: Itani
13.text: It is He who begins and repeats.

13.author: Maududi
13.text: He it is Who creates for the first time and He it is Who will create again,

13.author: Mubarakpuri
13.text: Verily, He it is Who begins and repeats.

13.author: Pickthall
13.text: Lo! He it is Who produceth, then reproduceth,

13.author: Qarai
13.text: It is indeed He who originates and brings back,

13.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
13.text: It is He who originates and repeats.

13.author: Saheeh International
13.text: Indeed, it is He who originates [creation] and repeats.

13.author: Sarwar
13.text: It is He who creates all things and causes them to return.

13.author: Shakir
13.text: Surely He it is Who originates and reproduces,

13.author: Wahiduddin Khan
13.text: it is He who begins and repeats [His creation],

13.author: Yusuf Ali
13.text: It is He Who creates from the very beginning, and He can restore (life).

14.author: Ahmed Ali
14.text: He is the forgiving and the loving,

14.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
14.text: And He only is the Oft Forgiving, the Beloved of His bondmen.

14.author: Arberry
14.text: and He is the All-forgiving, the All-loving,

14.author: Daryabadi
14.text: And He is the Fogiving, the Loving,

14.author: Hilali & Khan
14.text: And He is Oft-Forgiving, full of love (towards the pious who are real true believers of Islamic Monotheism),

14.author: Itani
14.text: And He is the Forgiving, the Loving.

14.author: Maududi
14.text: and He is the Ever Forgiving, the Most Loving

14.author: Pickthall
14.text: And He is the Forgiving, the Loving,

14.author: Qarai
14.text: and He is the All-forgiving, the All-affectionate,

14.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
14.text: And He is the Forgiving, and the Loving.

14.author: Saheeh International
14.text: And He is the Forgiving, the Affectionate,

14.author: Sarwar
14.text: He is the All-forgiving, the Most Loving One,

14.author: Shakir
14.text: And He is the Forgiving, the Loving,

14.author: Wahiduddin Khan
14.text: and He is the Forgiving and Loving One.

14.author: Yusuf Ali
14.text: And He is the Oft-Forgiving, Full of Loving-Kindness,

15.author: Ahmed Ali
15.text: The Lord of the glorious Throne,

15.author: Arberry
15.text: Lord of the Throne, the All-glorious,

15.author: Daryabadi
15.text: Lord of the Throne, the Glorious,

15.author: Hilali & Khan
15.text: Owner of the throne, the Glorious

15.author: Maududi
15.text: – the Lord of the Glorious Throne,

15.author: Mubarakpuri
15.text: Owner of the Throne, Al-Majid (the Glorious).

15.author: Pickthall
15.text: Lord of the Throne of Glory,

15.author: Qarai
15.text: Lord of the Throne, the All-glorious,

15.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
15.text: Owner of the Throne, the Exalted.

15.author: Sarwar
15.text: the Owner of the Throne, the Glorious One,

15.author: Shakir
15.text: Lord of the Arsh, the Glorious,

15.author: Wahiduddin Khan
15.text: The Lord of the Glorious Throne,

15.author: Yusuf Ali
15.text: Lord of the Throne of Glory,

16.author: Hilali & Khan
16.text: He does what He intends (or wills).

16.author: Maududi
16.text: the Executor of what He wills.

16.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
16.text: The Doer of whatever He wants.

16.author: Sarwar
16.text: and the Most Effective in His decision.

16.author: Shakir
16.text: The great doer of what He will.

16.author: Yusuf Ali
16.text: Doer (without let) of all that He intends.

17.author: Ahmed Ali
17.text: Has the story of the hordes

17.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
17.text: Did the story of the armies reach you?

17.author: Arberry
17.text: Hast thou received the story of the hosts,

17.author: Daryabadi
17.text: Hath there come Unto thee the story of the hosts-

17.author: Hilali & Khan
17.text: Has the story reached you of the hosts,

17.author: Itani
17.text: Has there come to you the story of the legions?

17.author: Maududi
17.text: Has the story of the armies reached you,

17.author: Mubarakpuri
17.text: Has the story reached you of the hosts.

17.author: Pickthall
17.text: Hath there come unto thee the story of the hosts

17.author: Qarai
17.text: Did you receive the story of the hosts

17.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
17.text: Has it come to you the story of the hosts

17.author: Saheeh International
17.text: Has there reached you the story of the soldiers -

17.author: Sarwar
17.text: Have you not heard about the stories of the armies

17.author: Shakir
17.text: Has not there come to you the story of the hosts,

17.author: Wahiduddin Khan
17.text: Have you not heard the story of the hosts

17.author: Yusuf Ali
17.text: Has the story reached thee, of the forces-

18.author: Ahmed Ali
18.text: Of Pharaoh and Thamud come to you?

18.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
18.text: (The armies of) Firaun and the tribe of Thamud.

18.author: Daryabadi
18.text: Of the Fir'awn and the Thamud?

18.author: Maududi
18.text: the armies of Pharaoh and Thamud?

18.author: Pickthall
18.text: Of Pharaoh and (the tribe of) Thamud?

19.author: Ahmed Ali
19.text: Yet the infidels persist in denial.

19.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
19.text: In fact the disbelievers are in denial.

19.author: Arberry
19.text: Nay, but the unbelievers still cry lies,

19.author: Daryabadi
19.text: But those who disbelieve are engaged in denial.

19.author: Hilali & Khan
19.text: Nay! The disbelievers (persisted) in denying (Prophet Muhammad (Peace be upon him) and his Message of Islamic Monotheism).

19.author: Itani
19.text: In fact, those who disbelieve are in denial.

19.author: Maududi
19.text: The unbelievers are indeed engaged in denying it, calling it a lie,

19.author: Pickthall
19.text: Nay, but those who disbelieve live in denial

19.author: Qarai
19.text: Indeed the faithless dwell in denial,

19.author: Saheeh International
19.text: But they who disbelieve are in [persistent] denial,

19.author: Sarwar
19.text: In fact, the disbelievers had always rejected (Our revelations).

19.author: Shakir
19.text: Nay! those who disbelieve are in (the act of) giving the lie to the truth.

19.author: Wahiduddin Khan
19.text: Yet those who deny the truth persist in denial.

19.author: Yusuf Ali
19.text: And yet the Unbelievers (persist) in rejecting (the Truth)!

20.author: Ahmed Ali
20.text: But God surrounds them from all sides.

20.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
20.text: And Allah is after them, has them surrounded.

20.author: Arberry
20.text: and God is behind them, encompassing.

20.author: Daryabadi
20.text: Whereas Allah is, from behind them, Encompassing.

20.author: Hilali & Khan
20.text: And Allah encompasses them from behind! (i.e. all their deeds are within His Knowledge, and He will requite them for their deeds).

20.author: Itani
20.text: And God encloses them from beyond.

20.author: Mubarakpuri
20.text: And Allah encompasses them from behind!

20.author: Pickthall
20.text: And Allah, all unseen, surroundeth them.

20.author: Qarai
20.text: and Allah besieges them from all around.

20.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
20.text: Allah encompasses them all from behind.

20.author: Saheeh International
20.text: While Allah encompasses them from behind.

20.author: Shakir
20.text: And Allah encompasses them on every side.

20.author: Wahiduddin Khan
20.text: God encompasses them from all sides.

20.author: Yusuf Ali
20.text: But Allah doth encompass them from behind!

21.author: Ahmed Ali
21.text: This is indeed the glorious Qur'an

21.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
21.text: In fact it (what they deny) is the Noble Qur’an.

21.author: Arberry
21.text: Nay, but it is a glorious Koran,

21.author: Daryabadi
21.text: Aye! it is a Recitation glorious,

21.author: Hilali & Khan
21.text: Nay! This is a Glorious Quran,

21.author: Itani
21.text: In fact, it is a Glorious Quran.

21.author: Maududi
21.text: Nay; but this is a glorious Qur'an,

21.author: Mubarakpuri
21.text: Nay! This is a Glorious Qur'an,

21.author: Pickthall
21.text: Nay, but it is a glorious Qur'an.

21.author: Qarai
21.text: Indeed it is a glorious Quran,

21.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
21.text: Indeed, this is a Glorious Koran,

21.author: Saheeh International
21.text: But this is an honored Qur'an

21.author: Sarwar
21.text: What is revealed to you is certainly a glorious Quran

21.author: Shakir
21.text: Nay! it is a glorious Quran,

21.author: Wahiduddin Khan
21.text: It is indeed a glorious Quran,

21.author: Yusuf Ali
21.text: Nay, this is a Glorious Qur'an,

22.author: Sarwar
22.text: that exists in a well-guarded tablet.
